I'm not sure if you're trolling or not, because there is nothing in the source code that supports what you're saying.

What you're basically saying they check for a blur event. That basically means they check for if another window gained focus, a lot of web based proctors use this technique.

HOWEVER, I tested this myself, and Respondus doesn't do that. I built a tool to check for blur events, I released grab from the VM, used my host machine, and the blur event never fired. The VM phyiscally **cannot  **"detect" when you tab out from it, I made sure of that.

Now I **WANT** to be optimistic that you might have some sort of special version of RLDB, but I doubt it, because you can only download RLDB through Respondus' website.

So either you have a subscription, it **DID ACTUALLY** work for you, and now you just want some free compensation by saying it doesn't work, or you don't have Evadus+ at all, and you're just trying to scare other users.

from what I can tell in the source code, it does the checks twice, on startup and when the test ends, so if you can start up RLDB, there's a *good chance* it will also pass the check when you hand in the test.
